rationale: Both papers are tightly aligned on conjoint preference measurement using self-stated importances and predictive validity, and the citing note explicitly frames a gap around "existing adaptive/hybrid methods" and SEM limitations. The cited paper is a major hybrid conjoint Bayesian method integrating stated and derived importances, which is plausibly a methodological/foundational prior for the citing paper’s positioning. With this topical and methodological overlap, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
